mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 0.5 percent month-over-month coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in greater than 70 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 228 signal entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 228.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real retail sales validates synchronized macroeconomic demand growth, reducing recession probabilities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive co-movement between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 indicates severe supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65 percent of periods where INDPRO drops below 95.0.
